title: Tapping to Celebrate Being a Wealth Steward
subtitle: A tapping practice for moving from seeing wealth as something to own and spend, to holding it with purpose and care
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 76 of a 12-week money programme. The script works with the older belief that wealth is something to accumulate and consume, and moves toward a confident, purposeful identity as someone who cares for and directs money for lasting good.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 76 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use when wealth feels like something to protect or consume rather than something to care for and direct toward meaningful ends.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 76 of a 12-week money programme. The script works with the view of wealth as something that is simply owned and spent, and moves toward an identity as someone who holds, guides, and directs money for lasting good.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though I once thought wealth was just for spending, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I didn't see myself as a steward, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I wasn't sure how to hold wealth with purpose, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I now see wealth as something to care for.
Side of eye: I'm not only an owner, I'm a guide.
Under eye: I shape abundance for future good.
Under nose: I direct money to meaningful work.
Chin: I love this responsibility.
Collarbone: I enjoy leading with wisdom and love.
Under arm: I honour the trust of this role.
Top of head: I celebrate being a true wealth steward.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I care for money with purpose and heart.
Side of eye: I guide resources to create lasting good.
Under eye: I let wealth benefit many lives.
Under nose: I feel grateful for this role.
Chin: I celebrate the impact of careful stewardship.
Collarbone: I use money to build freedom and joy.
Under arm: I love being trusted with great abundance.
Top of head: I honour my role as a wise wealth steward.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from Day 76 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
